subject: Anthropology
title: The Holistic Approach
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Holistic Approach.

hook.text: A doctor treats the symptom. An anthropologist asks about the diet, the gods, and the village chief.

buildup.text: Holism means studying all parts of human life as connected — economy, beliefs, kinship, ecology, and power.

discovery.text: You can't understand a wedding ritual without knowing the economy, religion, and politics that shape it.

twist.text: Specialists see details. Anthropologists see systems. That wide lens reveals patterns others miss entirely.

climax.text: Holism is anthropology's superpower: nothing human makes sense in isolation, only in its full context.

quiz.question: What does the holistic approach mean in anthropology?
options: Studying all parts of human life as connected
